metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=full driver=gap:wait_turn — 4 prompts in one session, each a clean ready→working→ready arc with no within-turn flicker; the antigravity parser keeps a turn working across all intermediate tool/text steps. wait_turn is a driver stub.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

## Reasoning

The scenario is a compressed proxy for a long session: 4 plain user prompts in one session, asserting per-turn `ready → working → ready` arcs with NO flicker WITHIN a turn, clean `working → ready` / `ready → working` transitions BETWEEN turns, and the state never stuck.

### agent_supports: yes

agy sustains a multi-turn conversation in one session: the `--continue` flag and the REPL both keep one `<conv-id>` across many prompts, each prompt opening a fresh turn. The real samples each show a multi-step turn with several intermediate assistant/tool lines before the terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE, so even plain prompts produce multi-chunk turns (the within-turn no-flicker invariant is meaningful).

### daemon_capability: full

The antigravity Source tails ONE `transcript.jsonl` per `<conv-id>`, appending across turns. Per turn, `parseUserInput` → `user_message` (`ready → working`); intermediate `assistant_message`/`function_call_output` lines are NON-settling (the turn stays `working`); the terminal empty `MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE` → `turn_done` (`working → ready`). There is no intermediate settle and no waiting path, so WITHIN each turn the session stays continuously `working` (no flicker), and BETWEEN turns the next `USER_INPUT` cleanly re-opens `working`. Four prompts produce the `ready → working → ready → working → ready → working → ready → working → ready` topology the scenario asserts, never stuck. Like the modern claudecode long-session cell, plain prompts keep the recording deterministic (and avoid any tool-permission stall), while each turn still has multiple intermediate chunks. → `daemon=full`.

### driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

The recipe sends 4 prompts, each needing `wait_turn` to block until that turn's terminal empty PLANNER_RESPONSE before sending the next (so the per-turn `working → ready` settle is observed cleanly, not merged with the next turn). `wait_turn()` is a driver stub (DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"). → `driver=gap:wait_turn`. `record` ports the wait_turn seam + the launch_repl session-resolution SEAM before driving. INTERACTIVE recipe (one continuous REPL session across all 4 turns).
caveats: Tokens/cost not in the antigravity JSONL; observations assert tokens_nonzero:false, cost_nonzero:false. Not a daemon bug.

Within-turn no-flicker is structural: the antigravity parser settles ONLY on the terminal empty PLANNER_RESPONSE; intermediate assistant_message/function_call_output are non-settling and there is no waiting path.

Provisional on emission: the per-turn shape is grounded in the parser read + samples (which show multi-step turns). record promotes provisional→settled, especially the clean between-turn working→ready→working edges across 4 turns on one continuous session.
driver=gap:wait_turn: 4× wait_turn is needed (one per turn) and wait_turn is a driver stub; record ports it + the launch_repl session-resolution SEAM first.

sources.note: parseUserInput → user_message (ready→working) opens each turn; intermediate assistant_message/function_call_output are non-settling; terminal empty PLANNER_RESPONSE → turn_done (working→ready); no waiting path → within-turn no-flicker, clean between-turn edges.
Real long agentic sample: one turn with 17+ intermediate planner/tool/result steps before the terminal empty PLANNER_RESPONSE — confirms multi-chunk turns and a single working span per turn.
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"; wait_turn() is a stub; the recipe needs 4× wait_turn (one per prompt) → driver=gap:wait_turn.
